title: Include zip code in location column + support LC filtering
excerpt: It would also be AMAZING if the Location column would automatically include zip code. I need to know mailing addresses, and zip codes are a must. If monday could detect and include that in the location, I will be SO happ…

posts.author: colleencass (Colleen Cassingham)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would also be AMAZING if the Location column would automatically include zip code. I need to know mailing addresses, and zip codes are a must. If monday could detect and include that in the location, I will be SO happy. Right now I have to have a whole other column for zip code, which is silly and takes up space.

Also, I often need to search my contact list to filter by city or state. It would be really helpful if I could filter by location, or at least if I typed “New York” into the search bar, it would bring up all the contacts geotagged in New York State. As is, they don’t appear in the list because the address only has “NY” in it.

posts.author: davidpriceav (David Price)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed! Zip code is a must for mailing/shipping. It should be a simple feat to add that into the column.

posts.author: matts (Matt S)
posts.cookedHtml:
We would love to see ZIP CODE included in suggested address results, too!

Right now, all suggested addresses end with “City, State, USA”.

A next level feature would be allowing the user to adjust the format of the suggested addresses in a “Column setting” menu.

posts.author: jcarbelbide (JC Arbelbide)
posts.cookedHtml: This would greatly help us to have the zipcode included in the location columns. Would also be nice to have a US state field if applicable. Will this ever be added? Would be great to have an official response about this.

posts.author: Dly215 (Duong )
posts.cookedHtml: Strange that this basic function isn’t included??

posts.author: kevinw (Kevin Wethington)
posts.cookedHtml: We created a new client intake form and use the address column so they can enter and select a verified address. However, the zip code does not populate on the selection and doesn’t carry over to the board once their form has been submitted. It would be nice to have the zip code automatically populate when entering the address.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there! We wanted to let you know that we will be merging this topic into this existing feature request - Include zip code in location column + support LC filtering - in order to consolidate matching threads and ensure votes are accurate and up-to-date. If you did not cast a vote on this topic, please add your vote to the active thread above. Thanks for your understanding

posts.author: HopeM (Hope McCorkle)
posts.cookedHtml: When can we expect to see the zip code in the location column?

posts.author: realperson (Kori Howell)
posts.cookedHtml: I need the zipcode! I shouldn’t have to go to Google to find the zipcode when Monday is already validating addresses. Please add the zipcode.

posts.author: HopeM (Hope McCorkle)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any update regarding the zip code? It would be really helpful if we had a zip code column.

posts.author: Briana (Briana Wagner)
posts.cookedHtml: Still a challenge. Had to use a separate numbers column and google to fill in the zip code for address - definitely not ideal

posts.author: cfrank (Frank)
posts.cookedHtml: we just published an integration on the marketplace that addresses this (it allows you to automatically rewrite monday locations to contain the zip code) and are planning to extend the scope as we learn more about your use cases. if you would like to give it a try: it’s called Location Plus

posts.author: loovanloon (Ton)
posts.cookedHtml: It is a pitty that this integration only supports US postal codes. I am looking for European (german, dutch, UK, France etc postal codes)

posts.author: loovanloon (Ton)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Briana Is there any ETA on extending the location column to support postal/zip codes?
I need this badly.

posts.author: cfrank (Frank)
posts.cookedHtml: we’d be happy to look into making that happen. there’s some complexity associated with automatic address enrichment globally (address formats can be different based on the country) which is why we focused on the US initially (and would gradually roll out new countries), but ultimately we’re building based on customer requirements

posts.author: MCautreels (Maarten Cautreels - Tryve)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have good news, we’ve just launched Location Toolkit as an app in the monday marketplace. We allow you to extract information from the Location column such as street, city, country as well as a postal code which we retrieve from Google Maps based on the location you’ve picked in the Location Column.

The app includes an integration which allows to extract the zip code from the Location column on a button click or whenever a specific column (for example the location column itself) changes.

Any feedback is much appreciated, feel free to get in touch!

posts.author: mchanna (Matt Hanna)
posts.cookedHtml: Kind of crazy to think that we’d have to pay extra to have zip code in addresses. Not like the zip code hasn’t been part of everyone’s address for 60 years.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: I don’t think address lookup and validation is nearly as simple as you assume it is.

posts.author: MCautreels (Maarten Cautreels - Tryve)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Matt,

I understand your frustration regarding the fact that you need an app to be able to extract a zip code. This is entirely up to monday.com to implement and they have lots of features on their backlog/roadmap.

In the meantime we’ve spent time to build an app on top of monday.com to close this gap. Our time is one of the reasons why this app is not free but there’s off course also a running cost to keep the app available as well as a cost to use the Google Maps API to retrieve the correct zip code for each of the locations.

If our app can save you 1 hour every month by automatically extracting things like the zip code from your location columns and thus save you 1 hour of your valuable time, I think it could be worth it.

And off course, the app does more then just extracting a zip code And we keep adding new features every few weeks!

Best,

Maarten

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml: Up voting this - we would love to use the Location column but cannot because it is too easy for someone to chose an address without a zip code. Would rather use zip code than USA at the end. Also frustrating we cannot convert a text column to a location column. The location column could be so much more useful!

posts.author: MCautreels (Maarten Cautreels - Tryve)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Rach12

We completely understand your frustrations and that’s why we’ve build Location Toolkit, an app for the monday.com marketplace.

It doesn’t only allow you to extract a zipcode from a Location column, it also has the ability to convert a text column containing an address into a Location column and many more features!

Check it out and try it for free for 14 days!

Best,

Maarten

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml: It is not ideal to have to pay just to have a zipcode listed. Unsure why Monday cannot do this. If you type in the full address you can actually select the zipcode, but it isn’t offered as an option unless you type the entire address, which defeats the purpose of the quick find ability. It seems this post has gotten thousands of views, @BiancaT is this on the monday.com roadmap at all?

posts.author: maryPPHC (Mary)
posts.cookedHtml: A client’s zip code is a basic piece of contact info any company collects from a customer for billing purposes and more. Not only does this cause all the same issues people have already mentioned above, but it causes breaks in company’s abilities to automate their processes, when a human has to go in and physically look up a zip code and add it. It’s not right for this to require a paid toolkit when this is one of the most basic pieces of information any industry is collecting. Looking forward to improvements to the location column.

posts.author: JamesK (James Kirk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Will the zip / post code ever be included by default?

  • I’m building a case to migrate my company to Monday.com and the lack of a zip/post code is one of the deciding factors.
    Baffling that such a basic function isn’t included by default.

posts.author: EG_TGR (Eric)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding this back to recent since there’s a few discussions about this. This is a must. At least to show a zip/postal for mailing, invoicing, etc.
